skills.name: systematic-debugging
skills.description: Structured debugging: reproduce, hypothesise, isolate, fix, verify. Returns to the current phase after resolution.

skills.name: brainstorming
skills.description: Ask clarifying questions, explore options, and get user approval before planning. Activated for Build intents.

skills.name: writing-plans
triggers: (plan( it)? out|write.*plan|implementation plan|break.?down|outline|task list|spec( it)? out)
skills.description: Break work into discrete tasks and confirm the plan with the user before execution begins.

skills.name: executing-plans
skills.description: Step-by-step execution of an approved plan. Triggered by explicit plan-continuation language.

skills.name: subagent-driven-development
skills.description: Delegate plan tasks to sub-agents for parallel implementation. Co-triggered with executing-plans.

skills.name: requesting-code-review
skills.description: Prepare and request a code review: summarise changes, highlight risks, open a PR.

skills.name: receiving-code-review
triggers: (review comments|pr comments|feedback|nits?|changes requested|address (the )?(review|comments|feedback)|respond to review|follow.?up review|re.?request review)
skills.description: Process incoming review feedback: address comments, push fixes, re-request review.

skills.name: verification-before-completion
skills.description: Final verification checklist before shipping: tests pass, lint clean, no TODOs, docs updated.

skills.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skills.description: Branch cleanup and merge: squash commits, update changelog, merge to main.

skills.name: openspec-ship
skills.description: Create retrospective OpenSpec change, validate, archive, update changelog

skills.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skills.description: Fan out independent tasks to parallel sub-agents and collect results.

skills.name: using-git-worktrees
skills.description: Use git worktrees for parallel branch work. Trigger-gated required: activates when parallel/isolation keywords match.

skills.name: writing-skills
skills.description: Author and maintain Claude Code skill files (SKILL.md). Meta-overlay for Claude tooling prompts.

skills.name: frontend-design
skills.description: UI/UX design guidance: component structure, responsive layout, styling, and visual prototyping.

skills.name: webapp-testing
skills.description: Web application testing: browser automation, accessibility checks, visual regression, and E2E flows.

skills.name: doc-coauthoring
skills.description: Co-author technical documents: proposals, specs, RFCs, and architecture docs.

skills.name: claude-automation-recommender
skills.description: Recommend Claude Code automation setups: hooks, MCP servers, plugins, and workflow optimisations.

skills.name: claude-md-improver
skills.description: Improve CLAUDE.md project files: structure, clarity, and completeness of project instructions.

skills.name: agent-team-execution
skills.description: Use when executing implementation plans with 3+ independent, file-disjoint tasks that benefit from parallel specialist agents.

skills.name: agent-team-review
skills.required_when: PR touches 3+ files, crosses module boundaries, or includes security-sensitive changes
skills.description: Multi-perspective parallel code review with specialist reviewers. Trigger-gated + condition-gated required.

skills.name: design-debate
skills.description: Multi-Agent Debate for complex designs. Spawns architect, critic, and pragmatist for collaborative design exploration.

skills.name: batch-scripting
skills.description: Bulk file operations using claude -p with manifest, dry-run, and log-based retry.

phase_guide.PLAN: writing-plans (break into tasks, confirm before execution)
phase_guide.IMPLEMENT: executing-plans or subagent-driven-development (offer agent-team-execution for 3+ independent tasks)
phase_guide.DEBUG: systematic-debugging, then return to current phase

methodology_hints.name: ralph-loop
methodology_hints.hint: RALPH LOOP: Consider /ralph-loop for autonomous iteration.

methodology_hints.name: pr-review
methodology_hints.hint: PR REVIEW: Consider /pr-review for structured review.

methodology_hints.name: unified-context-stack-hint
methodology_hints.hint: CONTEXT STACK: Use the unified-context-stack for tiered documentation retrieval. Query Context Hub via Context7 (libraryId=/andrewyng/context-hub) first for curated docs, then fall back to broad Context7, then chub CLI, then web search. Read the phase document for your current SDLC phase from the unified-context-stack skill (paths listed in the Context Stack session-start output).

methodology_hints.name: github-mcp
methodology_hints.hint: GITHUB: If GitHub MCP tools are available, check for related PRs, issues, and workflow runs. Reference any GitHub context already discussed in this session.

methodology_hints.name: atlassian-jira
methodology_hints.hint: ATLASSIAN: If Jira MCP tools are available, pull acceptance criteria and linked context for relevant tickets. Reference any Jira context already discussed in this session.

methodology_hints.name: atlassian-confluence
methodology_hints.hint: ATLASSIAN: If Confluence MCP tools are available, search for relevant design docs and references. Keep searches narrow by title or space.

methodology_hints.name: gcp-observability
methodology_hints.hint: GCP OBSERVABILITY: If observability MCP tools are available, verify runtime state. Scope queries to service + environment + narrow time window (30-60 min).

methodology_hints.name: gke
methodology_hints.hint: GKE: If GKE MCP tools are available, query cluster context and logs. Prefer scoped LQL queries over broad log dumps.

methodology_hints.name: cloud-run
methodology_hints.hint: CLOUD RUN: If Cloud Run MCP tools are available, check service state and recent logs.

methodology_hints.name: firebase
methodology_hints.hint: FIREBASE: If Firebase MCP tools are available, inspect resources read-only (documents, auth, rules). Use CLI for mutations.

methodology_hints.name: playwright-mcp
methodology_hints.hint: PLAYWRIGHT: If Playwright MCP tools are available, use them for visual verification and interactive debugging.

methodology_hints.name: frontend-playwright
methodology_hints.hint: PLAYWRIGHT TESTING: Frontend changes detected. Include Playwright E2E tests covering the changed UI behavior. Use the webapp-testing skill.

methodology_hints.name: claude-md-maintenance
methodology_hints.hint: CLAUDE.MD: If this session changed project conventions or structure, consider /revise-claude-md

methodology_hints.name: openspec-ship-reminder
methodology_hints.hint: OPENSPEC: After verification-before-completion passes, invoke openspec-ship to generate as-built documentation before committing. This is mandatory for feature shipping.

plugins.name: commit-commands
plugins.description: Structured commit workflows and branch-to-PR automation

plugins.name: security-guidance
plugins.description: Write-time security blocker for XSS, injection, unsafe deserialization

plugins.name: hookify
plugins.description: Custom rule authoring for Claude behavior guards

plugins.name: feature-dev
plugins.description: Full feature pipeline with parallel exploration and architecture agents

plugins.name: context7
plugins.description: Up-to-date library documentation via Context7 MCP. Resolves library IDs and fetches current docs, API references, and examples.

plugins.name: github
plugins.description: GitHub MCP for repository management. Create/review PRs, manage issues, search repos, and interact with GitHub APIs programmatically.

plugins.name: atlassian
plugins.description: Jira and Confluence integration via claude.ai managed MCP. Connect via /mcp in Claude Code. Pull acceptance criteria, search issues, read design docs.

plugins.name: unified-context-stack
plugins.description: Tiered context retrieval — curated docs (Context Hub via Context7), blast-radius mapping (Serena), institutional memory (Forgetful) with graceful degradation.

parallel.purpose: Parallel codebase exploration while brainstorming clarifies intent

parallel.use: tiered context retrieval (external docs, blast-radius, memory)
parallel.when: installed AND any context_capability is true
parallel.purpose: Gather curated API docs, map dependencies, check institutional memory during design

hints.text: Consider Skill(skill-creator) to eval/benchmark skill quality

parallel.use: tiered context retrieval (external docs, blast-radius, memory)
parallel.when: installed AND any context_capability is true
parallel.purpose: Gather curated API docs, map dependencies, check institutional memory before planning
Passive write-time security guard (always active, no explicit invocation)

parallel.when: installed AND any context_capability is true
parallel.purpose: Look up current API signatures and usage patterns during implementation
Write failing test first, then minimal code to pass. INVOKE before writing production code
Parallel specialist review agents (code quality, silent failures, simplification)
Read PR details and post review comments directly via GitHub API

parallel.use: tiered context retrieval (claim verification, dependency checks)
parallel.when: installed AND any context_capability is true
parallel.purpose: Verify reviewer claims against curated docs, check dependency impact
Scan for vulnerabilities, OWASP risks, compliance issues. INVOKE during every review

hints.text: Consider /review-pr for structured multi-agent PR review

sequence.purpose: Create retrospective OpenSpec change, validate, archive, update changelog

sequence.when: installed AND any context_capability is true
sequence.purpose: Consolidate learnings via chub annotate and/or memory-save before session close
Execute structured commit after verification passes
Branch cleanup, merge, or PR creation

sequence.when: installed AND user chooses PR option

hints.text: Use GitHub MCP tools (create_pull_request) for programmatic PR creation with labels and reviewers

parallel.use: tiered context retrieval (past solutions, live issue discovery)
parallel.when: installed AND any context_capability is true
parallel.purpose: Check past solutions via memory, and live library issues via curated docs
Reproduce bug with failing test before fixing. INVOKE before writing fix code
